ops.text: Confirmed from run 29723012477 --log-failed: exit code 124, "TIMEOUT: docs build exceeded the 300s hard cap — killing the quarto process tree ... GD_BUILD_TIMEOUT=<secs> overrides, 0 disables." The build reached step 19/19 (all steps started: 06:55:43 → 07:00:42 ≈ 299s) — no render/pandoc/quarto content error; the new cc_steer/instrument.py reference page rendered fine. Pure wall-clock: this repo's full API surface renders in ~285s locally (4m44.6s with GD_BUILD_TIMEOUT=0) and ~340s on the CI runner, both over gd-build's 300s default inner cap. Root cause = default inner cap set below the repo's legitimate render time; the outer job cap is timeout-minutes: 15. Fix authored in the cc-guides fragment .claude/fragments/.github/workflows/docs.yml/docs-build-preamble.fragment.yml (workflow-level env GD_BUILD_TIMEOUT="780", below the 15-min job bound so a genuine hang is still killed), re-rendered into .github/workflows/docs.yml. Note this is only a symptom mitigation for cc-steer; the durable platform fix (raise gd-build's default or make it runner-aware) is papercut 437b30e2.
